title: rubus chaerophyllus subsp. axillaris
intro: Rubus chaerophyllus subsp. axillaris, also known as a wild thimbleberry or a subspecies of the rubus chaerophyllus, is a type of flowering shrub that belongs to the rose family. The medicinal properties of Rubus chaerophyllus subsp. axillaris include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of consuming Rubus chaerophyllus subsp. axillaris include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Rubus chaerophyllus subsp. axillaris can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
